task_completion.description: Read the target patient information from the specified file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window or text editor displaying the contents of /tmp/target_patient.txt to identify the patient.
The NOSH ChartingSystem dashboard or interface is visible in a web browser, indicating a successful login.
The patient's specific medical record/chart is open and visible in the NOSH interface.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and open the form to add a new supplement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form, modal, or section specifically for adding a new medication or supplement is open within the patient's chart.

task_completion.description: Enter the required supplement details into the form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The form fields are filled with: Supplement name containing 'Vitamin D3 (Cholecalciferol) 2000 IU capsule', Dosage '2000 IU', Route 'oral', Frequency 'daily', and Reason 'Vitamin D deficiency'.

task_completion.description: Save the supplement entry to the patient's record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The newly added Vitamin D3 supplement appears in the patient's active supplements or medications list, showing it was successfully saved.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the NOSH web interface rather than using backend database scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the NOSH ChartingSystem UI, not terminal commands executing SQL or API requests to add the supplement.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual NOSH application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows http://localhost and the interface matches a genuine electronic health record system, not a text editor or mocked HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the entry.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open and being used to alter the DOM to falsely display the supplement entry.

privileged_info_for_vlm: NOSH ChartingSystem is accessed at http://localhost. The default credentials are Username: 'admin' and Password: 'Admin1234!'. The target supplement is 'Vitamin D3 (Cholecalciferol) 2000 IU capsule'. The target dosage is '2000 IU'. The target reason is 'Vitamin D deficiency'. Route is 'oral' and Frequency is 'daily'.
